easy.title: Word Formation: A Trip to the Mountains
easy.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Choose the correct answer (FCE-style, guided).
easy.passage: Last summer we had a _____ holiday in the Alps. The _____ was amazing, and the weather was _____ every day. We went walking in the mountains and the _____ from the top was incredible. The _____ we met were very friendly and gave us lots of _____ about places to visit. I would _____ the area to anyone who loves nature. The _____ of reaching the summit made all the climbing worthwhile.

gaps.explanation: Noun: view (what you see from the top).
Noun (plural): locals (people who live in the area).

easy.title: Word Formation: A Day at the Beach
easy.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE-style).
easy.passage: We had a _____ day at the beach. The _____ was clear and the sea was _____. The _____ of the waves was very relaxing. We met some _____ who were surfing. I would _____ taking a picnic. The _____ of the sunset was unforgettable.

gaps.explanation: Noun: the sound of the waves.
Noun: the beauty of the sunset.

easy.title: Word Formation: A New Hobby
easy.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE-style).
easy.passage: Learning to cook has been a _____ experience. At the _____, I found it difficult, but with _____ I improved. I enjoy cooking _____ because I can try new recipes. The _____ of a home-made meal is very satisfying. My family are very _____ and always try my dishes. I would _____ this hobby to anyone.

medium.title: Word Formation: Learning a New Language
medium.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of each line to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE Part 3).
medium.passage: Many people find it _____ to learn a new language as an adult. However, with _____ and regular practice, it is _____ possible to make progress. Some learners prefer to study _____, while others enjoy the _____ aspect of a class. There is no single _____ method; what works for one person may not work for another. The key is to stay _____ and to accept that _____ is not necessary to communicate well.

medium.title: Word Formation: Healthy Lifestyle
medium.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE Part 3).
medium.passage: A _____ lifestyle can improve both physical and _____ wellbeing. _____ exercise helps to reduce stress and increase _____ . Many people find that _____ or yoga is _____ for relaxation. _____ sleep is also essential. Making small changes can lead to _____ improvements in your quality of life.

medium.title: Word Formation: The Digital Age
medium.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (FCE Part 3).
medium.passage: The _____ of the internet has transformed the way we communicate. _____ means that we can connect with people around the world. However, _____ to social media can be a problem. It is important to find a _____ between online and offline life. _____ and security are growing concerns. _____ about safe internet use is essential for young people.

hard.title: Word Formation: The Future of Work
hard.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CAE Part 3).
hard.passage: The _____ of work is changing rapidly due to technology and globalisation. _____ patterns have shifted towards more flexible arrangements, and _____ working has become widespread. While this can lead to greater _____ for workers, it also raises questions about job _____ and the _____ of opportunities. _____ argue that not everyone has the _____ or skills to adapt, and that policymakers must address these _____ to ensure a fair transition.

hard.title: Word Formation: Climate Action
hard.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CAE Part 3).
hard.passage: The _____ of the climate crisis requires _____ action. _____ and businesses must work together to reduce _____ . _____ energy sources are becoming more _____ viable. _____ of natural habitats is essential for _____ development. _____ can also make a difference through their daily choices.

hard.title: Word Formation: Cultural Heritage
hard.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CAE Part 3).
hard.passage: The _____ of cultural heritage is important for future generations. _____ buildings and _____ crafts need _____ from neglect and development. _____ such as UNESCO work to promote _____ of the value of heritage. _____ of damaged sites requires _____ and funding. _____ communities often play a key role in maintaining _____ identity.

expert.title: Word Formation: Scientific Discovery
expert.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CPE Part 3).
expert.passage: Scientific _____ has always been driven by a combination of _____ and _____ concerns. While some research is motivated by _____ interest, much of it is funded with _____ or _____ support. The _____ between pure and applied science is complex, and _____ continue to debate the extent to which _____ considerations should influence the direction of research.

gaps.explanation: Noun as modifier: government support (standard phrase). Governmental is possible but less common here.

expert.title: Word Formation: Technological Innovation
expert.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CPE Part 3).
expert.passage: Technological _____ has accelerated at an _____ pace. _____ are constantly pushing the _____ of what is possible. _____ surrounding certain applications of AI has led to calls for _____ and _____ . _____ and ethicists debate the _____ of automation for human agency and _____ .

expert.title: Word Formation: Legal and Moral Responsibility
expert.instructions: Read the text below. Use the word given in capitals to form a word that fits in the gap. Use only one word in each gap (CPE Part 3).
expert.passage: The _____ of autonomous systems when they cause harm is a _____ issue. _____ in most countries has yet to catch up with _____ change. Some argue that _____ should extend to the designers of _____ that make _____ decisions. _____ bodies are considering _____ that would clarify _____ in such cases.
